document.title: Radiation Risks from Medical X-ray Examinations as a Function of the Age and Sex of the Patient
authorityOrJurisdiction: Centre for Radiation, Chemical and Environmental Hazards

sourceSummary.oneLine: HPA report evaluating radiation risks from medical x-ray examinations by patient age and sex using ICRP Publication 103 risk models and UK patient dose data.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical and regulatory risk discussion for medical x-ray exposure justification, optimisation, effective dose interpretation, cancer risk, and heritable-effect risk.
keyPoints: The report evaluates lifetime radiation-induced cancer risk and risk of deleterious heritable effects separately.
Risk estimates are based on ICRP Publication 103 risk models and typical organ doses for common UK x-ray examinations derived from Monte Carlo calculations and national patient dose surveys.
Effective dose should not be used to assess risks to individual patients of specific age, sex and nationality, but can compare doses and risks for a reference person.
The report proposes age- and sex-dependent risk coefficients by anatomical region: head, neck, chest, and abdomen and pelvis.
Typical cancer risk ranges from less than about 1 in a billion for knee or foot x-ray examinations to over 1 in 1,000 for a young girl having CT of the whole trunk.

entities.name: Centre for Radiation, Chemical and Environmental Hazards
entities.evidence: Centre for Radiation, Chemical and Environmental Hazards

entities.name: International Commission on Radiological Protection
entities.evidence: the International Commission on Radiological Protection has developed the concept of effective dose

entities.name: ICRP Publication 103
entities.evidence: risk models described in ICRP Publication 103

entities.name: UK Ionising Radiation (Medical Exposure) Regulations
entities.evidence: The UK Ionising Radiation (Medical Exposure) Regulations (IR(ME)R, 2000) require that the ‘practitioner’ shall be responsible for the justification of every medical exposure

concepts.name: Effective dose
concepts.evidence: Effective dose (E) provides a single measure of the dose to a reference person

concepts.name: Medical exposure justification
concepts.evidence: Justification requires that the medical benefits should outweigh the radiation risks

concepts.name: Radiation-induced cancer risk
concepts.evidence: The radiation-induced cancer risk was found to vary with patient age and sex

concepts.name: Heritable effects risk
concepts.evidence: risk of deleterious heritable effects appearing in the progeny of the patient

concepts.name: Age and sex dependent risk coefficients
concepts.pageTarget: Age and Sex Dependent X-ray Risk Coefficients
concepts.evidence: four sets of averaged age and sex dependent risk coefficients

concepts.name: Anatomical region risk grouping
concepts.evidence: grouping examinations within four anatomical regions (head, neck, chest and abdomen & pelvis)

evidence.claim: The document is HPA report HPA-CRCE-028 with approval and publication in October 2011.
The report title is Radiation Risks from Medical X-ray Examinations as a Function of the Age and Sex of the Patient.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiation Risks from Medical X-ray Examinations as a Function of the Age and Sex of the Patient
evidence.pageTarget: Radiation Risks from Medical X-ray Examinations as a Function of the Age and Sex of the Patient

evidence.claim: The report evaluates radiation risks from medical x-ray examinations by age and sex for cancer and heritable effects.
evidence.evidenceText: The radiation risks from medical x-ray examinations have been evaluated as a function of the age and sex of the patient in terms, separately, of the lifetime risk of radiation-induced cancer to the patient and the risk of deleterious heritable effects appearing in the progeny of the patient.

evidence.claim: The risk estimates use ICRP Publication 103 risk models and typical organ doses for common x-ray examinations.
evidence.evidenceText: These risks have been estimated on the basis of the risk models described in ICRP Publication 103, together with typical organ doses for a range of common x-ray examinations

evidence.claim: The report uses Monte Carlo calculations and UK radiology patient dose survey data.
evidence.evidenceText: derived by Monte Carlo calculation from patient dose data obtained in recent national surveys of UK radiology practice

evidence.claim: Effective dose is not intended for individual patient risk assessment by specific age, sex and nationality.
evidence.evidenceText: Effective dose ... should not be used to assess risks to individual patients of specific age, sex and nationality, an application for which it was not intended.

evidence.claim: IR(ME)R 2000 requires the practitioner to justify every medical exposure considering individual patient characteristics.
evidence.evidenceText: The UK Ionising Radiation (Medical Exposure) Regulations (IR(ME)R, 2000) require that the ‘practitioner’ shall be responsible for the justification of every medical exposure, taking into account the characteristics of the individual patient involved.

evidence.claim: The report proposes region-specific age and sex dependent risk coefficients for head, neck, chest, and abdomen and pelvis examinations.
evidence.evidenceText: grouping examinations within four anatomical regions (head, neck, chest and abdomen & pelvis) led to a further four sets of averaged age and sex dependent risk coefficients

evidence.claim: Typical cancer risk ranges from less than about 1 in a billion for knee or foot x-rays to over 1 in 1,000 for a young girl having CT of the whole trunk.
evidence.evidenceText: range from less than about 1 in a billion (<10-9) for any patient having an x-ray examination of the knee or foot, to over 1 in a 1,000 (10-3) for a young girl having a CT scan of the whole trunk

evidence.claim: For heritable effects, the report uses a rounded ICRP Publication 103 risk coefficient of 0.5% per Gy of gonadal dose for patients of reproductive capacity.
evidence.evidenceText: we shall assume a similarly rounded risk coefficient of 0.5% per Gy of gonadal dose for all male or female patients of reproductive capacity

reviewFlags.reason: No product model is identified in the source text; productModels is empty.
Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text identifies Health Protection Agency, UK, ICRP, BEIR, UNSCEAR, and IR(ME)R; no FDA authority content is present.
